mission.title: spire: [RFC] Deprecate SPIRE DataStore pluggability
mission.description: [RFC] Deprecate SPIRE DataStore pluggability. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify spire upgrade symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your spire deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n spire --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review spire configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl get configmap -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=spire
```
The DataStore plugin interface is large and complex. It frequently requires changes to support new behavior. Even though SPIRE has done well to maintain compatibility guarantees with other plugin types, it has not done so with the DataStore plugin.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [RFC] Deprecate SPIRE DataStore pluggability
steps.description:
See #1650 for background. As part of the fix, extends the PluginInfo interface to include information about whether or not the plugin is built in (to facilitate the newly added check).

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm [RFC] Deprecate SPIRE DataStore pluggability is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n spire --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n spire --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: See #1650 for background. As part of the fix, extends the PluginInfo interface to include information about whether or not the plugin is built in (to facilitate the newly added check).

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with spire installed or the issue environment reproducible.
